personas.name: Budget-Conscious Support Seeker
personas.description: A user who wants mental health support tools but must understand premium value before paying for access.
goals: Continue mood tracking without unexpected costs
Understand which features are free and which require premium
Pay only when the premium benefits are clear
pain_points: Core features feel locked after a short trial
Subscription value is unclear before purchase
Premium upgrades and refunds can feel risky
context: Trying the app during self-help or early therapy exploration
Comparing MindDoc with lower-cost or free alternatives
constraints: Needs transparent subscription information before purchase

personas.name: Structured Check-In User
personas.description: A user who depends on reminders and consistent daily check-ins to maintain a mood tracking habit.
goals: Complete morning, afternoon, and evening check-ins consistently
Recover missed check-ins without losing reflections
pain_points: Notifications are missing, too frequent, or easy to miss
Fixed reminder windows do not fit the user's routine
Entries can be lost when a time window closes
context: Balancing the app with work, school, or irregular schedules
Using short check-ins instead of long journal sessions
constraints: Reminder times must match the user's routine
Missed check-ins should not discard the user's input

personas.name: Therapy-Prepared Journaler
personas.description: A user who journals regularly and wants reports that support reflection and clinician conversations.
goals: Track mood and symptom patterns over time
Share clear progress summaries with a therapist or doctor
pain_points: Reports can feel generic or not actionable
Journal history is hard to review in one place
Insights do not always support therapy discussions
context: Using the app over weeks or months
Combining MindDoc with therapy, CBT, or self-monitoring
constraints: Reports must summarize tracked patterns clearly
Exported outputs must be usable outside the app

personas.name: Privacy and Continuity User
personas.description: A user storing sensitive mental health history who needs secure access, data continuity, and visible privacy information.
goals: Recover saved data after sign-in, reinstall, or device change
Understand how personal data is shared
pain_points: The app logs the user out or blocks access to past entries
Privacy settings and app lock behavior feel unreliable
Data access can break during login or connectivity issues
context: Using the app over long periods with sensitive mental health entries
Returning to the app after an update, reinstall, or device switch
constraints: Historical data must remain available after authentication
Privacy information must be visible in the app

personas.name: Nuanced Mood Tracker
personas.description: A user with complex or fluctuating feelings who needs more expressive check-ins than repetitive yes/no prompts.
goals: Reuse custom labels across future check-ins
pain_points: Emotion and activity labels are too limited
Repetitive prompts reduce engagement over time
context: Logging multiple emotional states during the day
Using the app for ongoing self-reflection rather than only screening
constraints: Needs customizable labels for recurring feelings and situations
Needs a non-binary response option for uncertain answers
